heading: Temporary endorsement for vessels procured outside the United States
label: 12115 Temporary endorsement for vessels procured outside the United States

children.heading: .—General Authority
children.label: a .—General Authority
children.text: The Secretary and the Secretary of State, acting jointly, may provide for the issuance of a certificate of documentation with an appropriate endorsement for a vessel procured outside the United States and meeting the ownership requirements of .section 12103 of this title

children.heading: .—Authorized Activity
children.label: b .—Authorized Activity
children.text: Subject to limitations the Secretary may prescribe, a vessel documented under this section may proceed to the United States and engage en route in foreign trade or trade with Guam, American Samoa, Wake, Midway, or Kingman Reef.

children.heading: .—Application of United States Jurisdiction and Laws
children.label: c .—Application of United States Jurisdiction and Laws
children.text: A vessel documented under this section is subject to the jurisdiction and laws of the United States. However, if the Secretary considers it to be in the public interest, the Secretary may suspend for a period of not more than 6 months the application of a vessel inspection law carried out by the Secretary or regulations prescribed under that law.

children.heading: .—Surrender of Certificate
children.label: d .—Surrender of Certificate
children.text: On the vessel’s arrival in the United States, the certificate of documentation shall be surrendered as provided by regulations prescribed by the Secretary.
